content.problemStatement:
Given an array of nnn integers : A1,A2,...,An A_1, A_2,... , A_nA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,...,An‚Äã, find the longest size subsequence which satisfies the following property: The xor of adjacent integers in the subsequence must be non-decreasing.

###Input:


First line contains an integer nnn, denoting the length of the array.
Second line will contain nnn space separated integers, denoting the elements of the array.
###Output:
Output a single integer denoting the longest size of subsequence with the given property.

###Constraints


1‚â§n‚â§1031 \leq n \leq 10^31‚â§n‚â§103
0‚â§Ai‚â§10180 \leq A_i \leq 10^{18}0‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§1018
###Sample Input:
8
1 200 3 0 400 4 1 7

###Sample Output:
6

###EXPLANATION:
The subsequence of maximum length is {1, 3, 0, 4, 1, 7} with Xor of adjacent indexes as {2,3,4,5,6} (non-decreasing)
